switching back to opus 4.6 due to codex outages. the difference for my workloads is stark. i don't trust opus at all to do a good job, specifically wrt to gathering all required context.

your biggest enemy is still complexity. it's also your agent's biggest enemy. but it has no holistic view of your code base, so it keeps adding complexity.

and you think that's how it's supposed to be, because the clanker shat it out, and you don't know the stack.

Your customer sees your product at $44.

They don't know if that's expensive or cheap.

So their brain asks one question:

"Does this LOOK like a $44 product?"

If your store looks like a $19 store, $44 feels like a ripoff.

If your store looks like a $90 store, $44 feels like a
